name: Proto-Peninsular Arabic
notes: Proto-Peninsular Arabic — abstract ancestor node for Hejazi, Najdi, Gulf, Yemeni, and Omani Arabic. Key shared peninsular features: interdentals often preserved; ق → /q/ or /ɡ/ (Bedouin); conservative consonant system. Emphatic (pharyngealization) spreading — low vowels back to [ɑ]/[ɑː] next to /tˤ dˤ sˤ ðˤ zˤ/ — is declared here as allophone_rules (AR_PEN_EMPH_*) and inherited by all Peninsular descendants.

sources.author: Watson, J.C.E.
sources.title: The Phonology and Morphology of Arabic

sources.author: Holes, C.
sources.title: Modern Arabic: Structures, Functions, and Varieties

allophone_rules.notes: Emphasis (pharyngealization) spreading: short /a/ backs to [ɑ] when it follows an emphatic consonant /tˤ dˤ sˤ ðˤ (zˤ)/. Watson 2002, The Phonology and Morphology of Arabic (OUP), ch. 'Emphasis', pp. 267-286: pharyngealization spreads bidirectionally within the phonological word, backing low vowels. Shared Peninsular feature; inherited by Hejazi, Najdi, Gulf, Yemeni and Omani via graphemes_base/OVERLAY_BY_ID.

Emphasis spreading (leftward): short /a/ backs to [ɑ] before an adjacent emphatic consonant. Watson 2002, The Phonology and Morphology of Arabic (OUP), ch. 'Emphasis', pp. 267-286: pharyngealization spreads bidirectionally within the phonological word, backing low vowels. Shared Peninsular feature; inherited by Hejazi, Najdi, Gulf, Yemeni and Omani via graphemes_base/OVERLAY_BY_ID.

Emphasis spreading: long /aː/ backs to [ɑː] after an adjacent emphatic. Watson 2002, The Phonology and Morphology of Arabic (OUP), ch. 'Emphasis', pp. 267-286: pharyngealization spreads bidirectionally within the phonological word, backing low vowels. Shared Peninsular feature; inherited by Hejazi, Najdi, Gulf, Yemeni and Omani via graphemes_base/OVERLAY_BY_ID.

Emphasis spreading (leftward): long /aː/ backs to [ɑː] before an adjacent emphatic. Watson 2002, The Phonology and Morphology of Arabic (OUP), ch. 'Emphasis', pp. 267-286: pharyngealization spreads bidirectionally within the phonological word, backing low vowels. Shared Peninsular feature; inherited by Hejazi, Najdi, Gulf, Yemeni and Omani via graphemes_base/OVERLAY_BY_ID.
